text:
Rebecca Adlington stole the show with her fashion choice while presenting the BBC'sOlympiccoverage on Friday night, leaving fans utterly captivated by her eye-catching dress.

Social media was ablaze with viewers eager to find out where they could buy the stunning linen maxi dress she donned.

Available from Boden and retailing for £175, the striking dress features a deep neckline, blouson sleeves and a multi-coloured pattern.

The ex-Olympic champion swimmer,who clinched two gold medalsin the 400-metre freestyle and the 800-metre freestyle events at the 2008 Olympics, received much acclaim for her stylish look.

ATwitteruser named Anna expressed: "I'm absolutely mesmerised by Rebecca Adlington's dress this evening. It's stunning. Her sense of style is wonderful."

READ MORE:BBC Breakfast fans left outraged as show suddenly ‘cuts off’ in shake-up



Echoing this sentiment, Jon chimed in: "Rebecca Adlington take a bow what a dress," while Anna joked: "Anyone else getting a #TheSimpsons vibe from Rebecca Adlington's dress tonight from the pool in #Paris2024? Definitely see Marge in there! Love it!"

Another noted: "As an aside to watching the elite @TeamGB athletes in the #Paris2024 Olympics I'm also enjoying Rebecca Adlington's dress collection. Today's is gorgeous," reportsBirmingham Live.

Twitter user Adrian gave a mixed review, writing: "Man, this dress Rebecca Adlington is wearing on #BBCOlympics I somehow both love it and hate it at the same time."

Rebecca has been broadcasting from the Olympic Aquatics Centre alongsideClare Baldingand fellow swimmer Mark Foster.

There were emotional scenes earlier this week whenAndy Murray's tennis career came to an end afterlosing to Team USA duo Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paulin the men's doubles.

As the 37-year-old exited the tennis alongside Dan Evans,Clare was left in floods of tearsand had to be consoled by Rebecca.

Reflecting on Murray's unforgettable career, Balding said: "My word, hasn't he shown us everything he is about in his three matches here.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

"It is never over until it's over. And now sadly, in terms of his professional playing career that is it, it is over. And all we can say is thank you, thank you."

Choking up, she turned to her punditry team and said: "You better say something," prompting Rebecca and Mark to take over the broadcast.

title: BBC Olympics fans say same thing as they're distracted by Rebecca Adlington | TV & Radio | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Leah Stanfield
description: Rebecca Adlington hosted BBC's coverage of the Paris Olympics on Friday night - and viewers all had the same reaction to her striking appearance.
